pr_title: feat(lint): finish governance ratchet — GC2/GC4/GC5 + GC6 hook
title: The `hasAdminTagInLeadingComments` function (L59–69 of `inngest-admin-tag.mjs`) calls `getAllComments()`, which returns every comment in the file, including those buried inside function bodies. A deve
body: The `hasAdminTagInLeadingComments` function (L59–69 of `inngest-admin-tag.mjs`) calls `getAllComments()`, which returns every comment in the file, including those buried inside function bodies. A developer can write `// @inngest-admin: cross-profile` inside an `if` block or helper function and the file-level check passes — defeating the point of a visible preamble declaration. Inline comment posted with a suggested cutoff fix. Test suite has no case that exercises a tag inside a function body, so this is currently unverified.

pr_title: feat(lint): finish governance ratchet — GC2/GC4/GC5 + GC6 hook
title: The JSDoc names the conditional-branch limitation but the same walk also descends into FunctionExpression / ArrowFunctionExpression nodes. A router.push(parent) inside a callback (e.g. a .forEach hand
body: The JSDoc names the conditional-branch limitation but the same walk also descends into FunctionExpression / ArrowFunctionExpression nodes. A router.push(parent) inside a callback (e.g. a .forEach handler or an onPress prop) would satisfy the check for the outer function even though that push never runs before the outer call. See inline comment for a one-line guard fix. Not blocking since 0 violations exist today, but worth closing before the rule catches real cross-stack pushes in review.

pr_title: feat(lint): finish governance ratchet — GC2/GC4/GC5 + GC6 hook
title: Drizzle exposes db.execute(sql) for raw SQL. This is the highest-risk form of DB access (no schema columns, no ORM query builder) but would silently bypass the tag requirement. See inline comment for
body: Drizzle exposes db.execute(sql) for raw SQL. This is the highest-risk form of DB access (no schema columns, no ORM query builder) but would silently bypass the tag requirement. See inline comment for a one-line addition. The GC5 sweep should also grep for db.execute sites.

pr_title: refactor(api): finish telemetry isolation sweep + forward-only ratchet
title: safe-non-core.guard.test.ts` — `isInsideTryBlock` comment contradicts implementation
body:
**3. `safe-non-core.guard.test.ts` — `isInsideTryBlock` comment contradicts implementation**

The comment claims "only the nearest enclosing try counts" but the code walks past all function boundaries. This creates a latent false-negative: an `inngest.send` inside an unawaited async callback nested in a try block would pass the ratchet even though the outer catch cannot receive its rejection. Fix the comment at minimum; stop at function boundaries for correctness.

---

pr_title: refactor(api): finish telemetry isolation sweep + forward-only ratchet
title: Files: apps/api/src/services/session/session-exchange.ts — processMessage catch (~line 1863) and both streamMessage catch blocks (~lines 2043, 2092)
body:
Files: apps/api/src/services/session/session-exchange.ts — processMessage catch (~line 1863) and both streamMessage catch blocks (~lines 2043, 2092)

The original code called captureException(persistErr, ...) unconditionally every time orphan persistence failed, giving Sentry a full exception object with stack trace. After this migration, Sentry only fires if the Inngest dispatch itself fails (inside safeSend). The underlying persistErr — the actual failure that triggered the orphan-persist path — is no longer sent to Sentry at all. Its .toString() string is embedded in the Inngest event payload, but that means no stack trace, no fingerprint, no queryable Sentry issue for orphan-persist failures.

CLAUDE.md: 'Silent recovery without escalation is banned.' The Inngest event satisfies the escalation requirement, but the Sentry observability regression is real. Each of the three sites should add an explicit captureException(persistErr, { extra: { phase: 'orphan_persist_failed', ... } }) call alongside safeSend. See the inline comment on lines 1863-1878 for a suggested diff.

---

pr_title: refactor(api): finish telemetry isolation sweep + forward-only ratchet
title: See inline comment on `safe-non-core.guard.test.ts` lines 99-119. The opening comment says "Stop at function boundaries" but the if-block is empty - the walk unconditionally continues across `ArrowFun
body: See inline comment on `safe-non-core.guard.test.ts` lines 99-119. The opening comment says "Stop at function boundaries" but the if-block is empty - the walk unconditionally continues across `ArrowFunction`/`FunctionDeclaration` ancestors. An `inngest.send()` inside a nested arrow function defined inside a try-catch passes the ratchet as `try-catch` even though the outer try does not guard its actual execution. Fix: add `return false;` inside the if-block.

pr_title: refactor(api): finish telemetry isolation sweep + forward-only ratchet
title: See inline comment on `session-exchange.ts` lines 1863-1879 (same pattern at the `stream` and `fallback` catch sites). The original code reported `persistErr` directly to Sentry with a full stack trac
body: See inline comment on `session-exchange.ts` lines 1863-1879 (same pattern at the `stream` and `fallback` catch sites). The original code reported `persistErr` directly to Sentry with a full stack trace AND dispatched an Inngest event. The new code only dispatches the Inngest event; `captureException` now fires only if the dispatch itself fails. If Inngest is unavailable, `persistErr` disappears from Sentry entirely. Per CLAUDE.md "Silent recovery without escalation is banned" - the Inngest event satisfies the structured-event requirement, but losing direct Sentry escalation for these three error-within-error catch sites is a regression. Suggest adding `captureException(persistErr)` before `safeSend` at all three orphan-persist sites.

pr_title: test(api): GC1 internal mock cleanup — API-side batches (#9, API only)
title: waitForEvent timeout` test: spy restored before assertion** (`session-completed.integration.test.ts:1337-1348`)
body:
**`waitForEvent timeout` test: spy restored before assertion** (`session-completed.integration.test.ts:1337-1348`)

`captureExceptionSpy.mockRestore()` is called at line 1337. Jest's `mockRestore()` chains through `mockReset()` then `mockClear()`, which zeroes out `mock.calls`. The `expect(captureExceptionSpy).toHaveBeenCalledWith(...)` at line 1348 then reads an empty call list and will always fail. See inline comment for a one-line fix (swap the restore and assertions).

This would not have surfaced in the 3,941-test unit count: `.integration.test.ts` files are excluded from the main jest run by `testPathIgnorePatterns`.

---

pr_title: test(api): GC1 internal mock cleanup — API-side batches (#9, API only)
title: Spy leak in `memory dedup rollout` test** (`session-completed.integration.test.ts:1239-1272`)
body:
**Spy leak in `memory dedup rollout` test** (`session-completed.integration.test.ts:1239-1272`)

`dedupEnabledSpy` and `rolloutSpy` are inline-restored after `handler()`. If `handler()` throws, both spies remain active (`.mockReturnValue(true)`) for all subsequent tests. `jest.clearAllMocks()` in `beforeEach` clears call counts but does NOT restore spy implementations — so a flaky upstream failure would silently corrupt the dedup-rollout flag for every later test. Wrap in `try/finally` or add `afterEach(() => jest.restoreAllMocks())` to the outer describe. See inline comment.

---

pr_title: test(api): GC1 internal mock cleanup — API-side batches (#9, API only)
title: In `session-completed.integration.test.ts`, two tests restore spies **inline inside the test body** rather than in an `afterEach`/`finally` block:
body:
In `session-completed.integration.test.ts`, two tests restore spies **inline inside the test body** rather than in an `afterEach`/`finally` block:

- **"memory dedup rollout"** (lines 1239–1272): `dedupEnabledSpy` and `rolloutSpy` are set up before the handler call but restored *after* the assertions. A failing `expect` at line 1275–1278 leaves both spies alive. Subsequent tests that depend on the real feature-flag values will see `true`, potentially masking real failures.
- **"waitForEvent timeout"** (lines 1305–1337): `captureExceptionSpy` has the same problem — restored after the assertions, not in cleanup.

The `routeAndCallSpy` in the same file is correctly placed in `beforeEach`/`afterEach`. Apply the same pattern here, or at minimum wrap with `try/finally`. Inline comments added.

pr_title: feat(ci): mobile-test verification receipts + pre-push gate
title: Both scripts fall back to the literal string main when both merge-base calls fail (verify-test-receipts.sh lines 30-32, record-test-receipt.sh lines 66-68). If a developer clone has neither origin/mai
body:
Both scripts fall back to the literal string main when both merge-base calls fail (verify-test-receipts.sh lines 30-32, record-test-receipt.sh lines 66-68). If a developer clone has neither origin/main nor a local main branch (renamed default, shallow clone, detached-HEAD CI environment), then git diff silently produces empty output: 2>/dev/null suppresses the unknown-revision error and || true swallows the exit code. changed becomes empty, the scope is skipped, and the gate is bypassed — the push goes through without a receipt.

Fix: exit non-zero when the base cannot be resolved instead of silently allowing an empty diff. See inline comment on verify-test-receipts.sh line 30.

---

pr_title: feat(ci): mobile-test verification receipts + pre-push gate
title: record-test-receipt.sh writes verifiedBy containing the git username and machine hostname into .test-receipts/mobile.json, which is committed and pushed. The machine hostname is visible to everyone wi
body:
record-test-receipt.sh writes verifiedBy containing the git username and machine hostname into .test-receipts/mobile.json, which is committed and pushed. The machine hostname is visible to everyone with repo read access and adds nothing the verifiedAt timestamp does not already provide. Consider dropping the hostname clause or replacing it with a truncated hash. See inline comment on record-test-receipt.sh line 116.

---

pr_title: feat(ci): mobile-test verification receipts + pre-push gate
title: The hook header documents two bypass paths (SKIP_RECEIPT_CHECK=1 git push and git push --no-verify), but the error block shown to the developer at runtime only mentions SKIP_RECEIPT_CHECK=1. A develop
body:
The hook header documents two bypass paths (SKIP_RECEIPT_CHECK=1 git push and git push --no-verify), but the error block shown to the developer at runtime only mentions SKIP_RECEIPT_CHECK=1. A developer who discovers --no-verify independently may use it without realising it bypasses all hooks silently and leaves no shell-history trace. Either mention both options explicitly so the developer makes an informed choice, or drop --no-verify from the header comment and rely solely on SKIP_RECEIPT_CHECK (which is traceable).

---

pr_title: fix(parent): restore child dashboard data surfaces
title: GC6: net-new internal mock added while editing test file
body:
**1. GC6: net-new internal mock added while editing test file**

`index.test.tsx` was edited and a new `jest.mock('../../../../hooks/use-progress', ...)` was added. GC6 requires _reducing_ internal mock sites on every test-file edit, not trading one for another. The `gc1-allow` annotation satisfies the lint ratchet (GC1) but GC6 goes further: each touch of a test file should chip down the legacy backlog.

`useProfileSessions` is called only inside `RecentSessionsList`, which is now a self-contained component. The test could mock `RecentSessionsList` itself instead of the underlying hook, or render it with a `QueryClient` and a seeded cache. Either satisfies both GC1 and GC6.

If the mock is genuinely unavoidable, record the deferral in the commit message (file path + count) as GC6 requires.

---

pr_title: fix(parent): restore child dashboard data surfaces
title: Imports split by section banner in `test-seed.test.ts
body:
**2. Imports split by section banner in `test-seed.test.ts`**

The new `fs`, `path`, `ENGAGEMENT_SIGNALS`, and `Database` imports appear before the `// Test Seed Service — Unit Tests` block comment, while `import { seedScenario, ... }` appears after it: imports → banner → more imports. Move the banner above all imports, or group all imports together below it.

pr_title: fix(parent): restore child dashboard data surfaces
title: fs.readFileSync` in the engagement-signal meta-test is fragile
body:
**3. `fs.readFileSync` in the engagement-signal meta-test is fragile**

`path.join(__dirname, 'test-seed.ts')` breaks silently if either file is moved. A more resilient alternative: export the engagement-signal list (or a whitelist constant) from `test-seed.ts` and import it directly in the test — same coverage, no filesystem dependency.

---

pr_title: feat(hooks): add blocking pre-push hook with delta-based validation
title: nx affected -t test --base=HEAD --exclude=mobile
body:
File: `scripts/pre-push-tests.sh`
Line: L133
Issue: `nx affected -t test --base=HEAD --exclude=mobile` — when pre-push runs, all commits are already in HEAD. `--base=HEAD` diffs HEAD against itself, finds zero changed files, and silently reports success. The fallback that is supposed to run broad tests for large deltas (>100 TS files) is effectively a no-op and **fails open**.
Rule: CLAUDE.md § Required Validation — "Do not call work complete if related tests, lint, typecheck, or required migrations are still failing." / broken functionality
Suggested Fix: Replace `--base=HEAD` with a meaningful base. The simplest fix that matches the surrounding delta logic: pass the computed remote SHA or `origin/main`. E.g. `NX_DAEMON=false pnpm exec nx affected -t test --base=origin/main --head="$local_sha" --exclude=mobile` — or capture the base SHA computed in Phase 1 and pass it here.

pr_title: feat(hooks): add blocking pre-push hook with delta-based validation
title: The template enforced three sections CLAUDE.md explicitly mandates: Verified-By (with exact command + result), Failure modes considered (CLAUDE.md § UX Resilience Rules
body:
File: `.github/pull_request_template.md`
Line: (deleted)
Issue: The template enforced three sections CLAUDE.md explicitly mandates: **Verified-By** (with exact command + result), **Failure modes considered** (CLAUDE.md § UX Resilience Rules — "Spec failure modes before coding. Every feature spec / story must include a Failure Modes table"), and **Sweep audit** (CLAUDE.md § Fix Development Rules — "Never silently fix one of N — document a deferred sweep with a tracked ID, owner, and target date"). Removing the template removes the one structural UI nudge that made these easy to fill. The PR description gives no explanation and introduces no replacement mechanism.
Rule: CLAUDE.md § UX Resilience Rules; § Fix Development Rules
Suggested Fix: Restore the template, or replace it with a lighter-weight version that retains the three required sections. If the intent is to remove it intentionally, explain in the PR description what replaces its enforcement (e.g., a commitlint rule, CI step).

pr_title: feat(hooks): add blocking pre-push hook with delta-based validation
title: File is silently deleted with no mention in the PR description.
body:
File: `.github/pull_request_template.md`
Line: (deleted)
Issue: File is silently deleted with no mention in the PR description. The template enforced the **Verified-By** checklist (which CLAUDE.md § Required Validation calls essential — citing PR #257 as the incident where skipping it shipped 189 broken tests), **Failure modes considered**, and **Sweep audit** sections. Removing it eliminates the structured prompt that keeps contributors honest about local verification and failure mode analysis. If the new blocking pre-push hook makes the receipt-based Verified-By step redundant, that reasoning belongs in the PR description and the template should either be updated to reflect the new workflow or explicitly retired with rationale.
Rule: CLAUDE.md § Required Validation; CLAUDE.md § UX Resilience Rules ("Spec failure modes before coding")
Suggested Fix: Either restore the template with wording updated to reflect the new hook (replace the receipt step with a note that the pre-push hook now validates mobile tests directly), or add a commit/PR description explaining why the full template is being retired rather than updated.

pr_title: feat(hooks): add blocking pre-push hook with delta-based validation
title: The template contained three sections not replaced by the blocking hook: ## Failure modes considered, ## Sweep audit, and ## Notes for reviewers.
body:
File: `.github/pull_request_template.md`
Line: N/A (file deleted)
Issue: The template contained three sections not replaced by the blocking hook: `## Failure modes considered`, `## Sweep audit`, and `## Notes for reviewers`. The blocking hook automates `tsc` and `jest`, making the `Verified-By` checkboxes redundant — but failure mode documentation and sweep audit evidence are not automated. CLAUDE.md explicitly requires a Failure Modes table (State / Trigger / User sees / Recovery) for every feature/fix, and a grep-backed sweep audit for any PR claiming a sweep. Removing the template silently drops these prompts with no replacement. The PR description does not call out this deletion as intentional.
Rule: CLAUDE.md § UX Resilience Rules ("Spec failure modes before coding"), § Fix Development Rules ("Sweep when you fix")
Suggested Fix: Restore the template; remove the now-automated `Verified-By` checkbox rows (`tsc --build`, lint, jest) since the hook enforces them; keep `## Failure modes considered`, `## Sweep audit`, and `## Notes for reviewers`.
